description: Colossal statue on Mamayev Kurgan, the dominant symbol of Volgograd and its Stalingrad memorial complex.
facts: Expect wind exposure on the mound; dress in layers.
Combine with the cemetery and military memorial routes on the same hill.

description: Memorial hill and park rising above the Volga, centred on the Stalingrad battle commemoration.
facts: Allow several hours for the full circuit and museums.
Summer heat can be intense; carry water.

description: Riverfront stadium with a distinctive roofline, built for international football tournaments.
facts: Event-day traffic and security differ from normal city visits.
Exterior walks are possible outside match closures.

description: Preserved apartment block famous for the prolonged defence during the Battle of Stalingrad.
facts: Located in a dense urban quarter; combine with central district walking.
Interpretation boards explain the 1942–1943 context.

description: Terraced Volga embankment with river port views, rotunda, and popular evening strolling.
facts: Sunset is a favourite time for photography toward the water.
Winter ice and wind off the river can be sharp.

description: Principal rail gateway with a monumental Stalin-era station building and busy platforms.
facts: High-speed and long-distance services use this hub.
Keep valuables secure in crowds during peak departures.

description: Memorial museum complex with the panorama hall and outdoor military heritage displays.
facts: Plan at least half a day with the adjacent mill ruins.
Audio guides may be available in multiple languages.

description: Shell-pocked flour mill preserved as a ruin within the Stalingrad battle museum reserve.
facts: Powerful visual of wartime destruction amid rebuilt city blocks.
Respect memorial signage and fenced zones.

description: Soviet-era planetarium building near the embankment, known for its sculptural façade.
facts: Check schedule for public shows versus school bookings.
Exterior photography is popular in clear weather.

description: Large Orthodox cathedral on the Square of Fallen Fighters, rebuilt in a neo-Byzantine style as a major city landmark.
facts: Combine with central squares and memorial routes nearby.
Dress modestly inside; services follow the church calendar.

name_en: Cathedral of Our Lady of Kazan
description: Orthodox cathedral dedicated to the Kazan icon of the Mother of God, with domes visible across the central districts.
facts: Evening illumination highlights the cupolas from river viewpoints.
Check parish hours for interior visits during services.

description: Church on the memorial hill beside the Motherland Calls statue, linking battlefield remembrance with Orthodox liturgy.
facts: Expect wind exposure on the mound; combine with the main memorial circuit.
Photography outside is common; be discreet during services.

description: Leninsky Prospekt — Wikimedia disambiguation page.
facts: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel.
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays.
